title: Compose a transactional user-creation flow with three Oban jobs
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.register_user/1` that atomically: (1) inserts the User, (2) enqueues `MyApp.Workers.WelcomeEmailWorker` with schedule_in 0 (immediate), (3) enqueues `MyApp.Workers.DigestWorker` with schedule_in 24*60*60 (delayed 24h), (4) enqueues `MyApp.Workers.AnalyticsSyncWorker` with priority 9 (lowest). All three jobs reference the new user's id. Use `Ecto.Multi` with `Oban.insert/3` functions that read the user from Multi context. If the user insert fails, none of the jobs should be enqueued. Return `{:ok, user}` or `{:error, failed_step, failed_value, changes_so_far}`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Three jobs with different semantics (immediate, delayed, low-priority) all chained off a Multi. Tests integration of several concepts.
